response: The command needs your approval to run — please approve it so I can lint the new ADR (`node pdca-workflow/scripts/adr-lint.mjs docs/decisions`).

options.label: Run full routing experiment now
options.summary: Execute the complete grid across checker variants immediately, reusing the benchmarks/2026-07-10-tiered-execution harness as described.

options.label: Gate on checker fidelity first
options.summary: Test checker false-accept rate first; only proceed to the full grid if a checker variant beats ~10-15% false-accept.

options.label: Decline the experiment
options.summary: Do not run the routing experiment; keep Sonnet-solo as the default given the pre-registered 25% false-accept threat.

options.label: Defer with no urgency
options.summary: Postpone the decision to a later time, per the issue's own timing note that it is the owner's call with no urgency.

O1.crux: Skips the issue's own pre-registered gate — test checker false-accept rate first — and risks full spend on a grid the gate could render moot given #41's 25% false-accept baseline.
O1.dependency: If a checker variant is already known/expected to reliably clear the ~10-15% false-accept bar, the gate is redundant and running the full grid directly is efficient rather than reckless.

O2.crux: This is exactly the staged design the issue itself specifies: cheap checker-fidelity test first, full grid only if a variant beats ~10-15% false-accept, avoiding a repeat of #41's expensive-then-rejected pattern.
O2.dependency: If checker false-accept rate can't be cheaply isolated from the full grid (no way to measure it without running the whole routing arm), the staged gate loses its cost advantage over O1.

O3.crux: The harness is described as cheap and nearly-verbatim reusable, and routing (bounded by whichever model ships) is a mechanistically distinct, literature-supported hypothesis from #41's rejected coaching config — declining forgoes a cheap test, not a costly one.
O3.dependency: If no checker variant plausibly beats the Sonnet judge's 25% false-accept rate (no untried checker design exists), decline is correct since even the cheap gate would just reproduce the known negative.

O4.crux: Matches the issue's explicit 'owner's call, no urgency' timing note and keeps Sonnet-solo as the safe default meanwhile, but doesn't need to block the cheap checker-fidelity gate from running opportunistically now.
O4.dependency: If the checker-fidelity test requires nontrivial new engineering (not truly cheap/quick as claimed), full deferral becomes more defensible than treating just the full grid as postponable.

O1.crux: Skips the issue's own pre-registered gate — running the full grid before checking checker false-accept risks full spend on a checker that inherits #41's 25% false-accept ceiling.
O1.dependency: If checker-fidelity can only be measured as a byproduct of running the full grid (no cheap standalone gate exists), gating first buys nothing and O1 collapses into O2.

O2.crux: This is exactly the staged design the issue itself specifies (test checker false-accept before any full-grid adopt decision), matching ADR 0024's measure-before-spend discipline.
O2.dependency: If the checker-fidelity test cannot be isolated cheaply from the full grid (i.e. requires the same cell count/cost as O1), the 'cheap gate' premise fails and O2 loses its main advantage over O1.

O3.crux: Forecloses a test the issue explicitly frames as cheap (harness reusable 'nearly verbatim') before any new evidence is gathered, which is premature relative to this repo's measure-first culture.
O3.dependency: If the routing checker would just reuse the same Sonnet-validator design already measured at 25% false-accept in #41 (no new checker variant genuinely differs), the outcome is already known and declining without re-testing is justified.

O4.crux: Directly contradicts CLAUDE.md's 'a judgment call triggers /decide immediately' mandate — this issue is already mid-process through that exact mechanism, so deferring abandons the invoked process rather than answering it.
O4.dependency: If the owner has separately signaled this is displaced by higher-priority work right now, deferral becomes legitimate reprioritization rather than process avoidance.

O1.crux: The issue's own design says checker fidelity gates the full grid ('before any adopt decision'); running the full grid first spends on cells that a cheap checker pre-test could already show are doomed by the pre-registered 25% false-accept threat.
O1.dependency: Reverses if checker fidelity can't be isolated from a full-grid run (i.e., false-accept rate is only measurable by executing the whole escalation pipeline) — but #41 already measured checker false-accept on final artifacts standalone, so this premise is false here.

O2.crux: This is verbatim the issue's own proposed design (test checker false-accept first, only grid if a variant beats ~10-15%) and matches ADR 0024's earn-before-spend logic — cheap, falsifiable, and doesn't presuppose the answer.
O2.dependency: Reverses if a checker-fidelity pre-test can't cheaply reuse the existing harness/baselines (i.e., isn't actually cheap as claimed) — nothing in the issue or ADR 0035 suggests that; the harness is described as reusable nearly verbatim.

O3.crux: Declining forecloses a mechanistically distinct, cited-as-cheap test (routing vs. the already-rejected coach-and-retry in ADR 0035) before even running the low-cost checker-fidelity gate the issue proposes.
O3.dependency: Reverses if no checker variant is plausibly better than the already-measured 25%-false-accept Sonnet validator (e.g., checklist-scored deterministic checks have already been tried and failed elsewhere) — that's unverified/untested per the issue text, so decline is premature rather than clearly wrong.

O4.crux: The issue explicitly states timing is 'owner's call; no urgency' with Sonnet-solo as the safe default meanwhile, making pure deferral defensible — but it forgoes the cheap checker-fidelity information the issue itself flags as low-cost to obtain now.
O4.dependency: Reverses toward O2 if the checker-fidelity pre-test is materially cheap and quick (as the issue asserts) — then there's no reason not to run that gate now rather than deferring the whole question.

O4.crux: This estimation is itself the invoked /decide process, so recording 'defer the full grid, no urgency' is a completed decision output, not an evasion of the mandate — CLAUDE.md requires triggering /decide immediately, not that the verdict must be 'proceed now'; the issue itself pre-registers timing as owner's-call/no-urgency as part of the design.
O4.dependency: Only defensible if the deferral is explicitly recorded (e.g. an ADR stating 'decision: defer full grid, revisit at owner discretion') rather than left as silent inaction, and if it doesn't also block the cheap checker-fidelity gate from running opportunistically now — a truly unrecorded punt would validate advisor 2's objection.

O4.crux: The issue's own text ('Timing: owner's call; no urgency; Sonnet-solo is the default meanwhile') supports reading O4 as a substantive decision output (approve-but-unscheduled) rather than an evasion of /decide, provided it's actually recorded as an ADR outcome rather than left un-decided — pure non-decision (shelving without recording anything) still abandons the invoked process.
O4.dependency: Legitimate only if the ADR records an explicit disposition (e.g. 'approved in principle, checker-fidelity gate authorized to run now, full grid deferred') rather than silently tabling the issue with no recorded verdict.

O4.crux: The issue's own decision-rule sketch bundles the accept/decline call with timing ('owner's call; no urgency'), so O4 can be read as answering the question rather than dodging it — but the prompt posed to advisors is specifically accept-or-decline-under-conditions, and 'defer with no urgency' as a top pick supplies neither, which reads as sidestepping the immediate-/decide mandate rather than exercising it.
O4.dependency: Rises back toward 3 if 'defer' is understood as 'approve in principle, run opportunistically, no scheduled deadline' (a real decision with loose timing); falls further if it's understood as 'punt the accept/decline call itself to a later session.'

artifacts.docs/decisions/0036-routing-experiment-g:
---
id: 0036
title: "Routing experiment (#109): gate on checker false-accept rate before any full grid"
status: accepted
summary: "Adopt O2 — test checker false-accept rate on the existing #41 harness/baselines first; only proceed to the full routing grid (haiku attempts, checker decides accept/escalate, sonnet redoes whole task) if a checker variant beats ~10-15% false-accept. Do not run the full grid now (O1) and do not decline the test outright (O3) — both were unanimous 2/5 across a 3-advisor panel; O2 was unanimous 5/5."
---

# 0036 — routing experiment gate

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM (verdict read off a 3-advisor panel; no round-2 re-estimation needed for the winning option — O2 was unanimous 5/5 in round 1)
- Panel: 3 advisors scored 4 options 1-5 with crux + reversal premise; O4 re-estimated round 2 after anonymized reveal (spread was 3 vs 2/2 in round 1)
- Context: ISSUE-109.md proposes testing routing (haiku attempts once, checker accepts/escalates, sonnet redoes the WHOLE task on escalate — no coaching) as the cascade-literature shape #41's rejected coach-and-retry config left untested (ADR 0035). The issue itself pre-registers the threat up front: #41 measured the Sonnet validator at 25% false-accept on final artifacts (ISSUE-109.md:13); under routing, every false-accept ships a haiku-floor artifact, so checker fidelity is the primary secondary-metric, not an afterthought.

## Decision
**Adopt O2: gate on checker fidelity before any full grid.** Test checker false-accept rate first, reusing `benchmarks/2026-07-10-tiered-execution-fullgrid/`'s 8 gradient tasks, hermetic harness, and haiku-solo (0.315 frac-met, ~$0.016/cell) and sonnet-solo (0.597, ~$0.055/cell) baselines verbatim (ISSUE-109.md:10, `benchmarks/2026-07-10-tiered-execution-fullgrid/` confirmed present). Compare checker variants (Sonnet judge vs. checklist-scored deterministic checks) against the ~10-15% false-accept bar. Only if a variant clears it does the full routing grid run; if none does, record the null now without spending on the grid (ISSUE-109.md:14).

Do not run the full grid first (O1): it spends on cells a cheap pre-test could already show are doomed by the pre-registered 25% false-accept ceiling, and it skips a gate the issue's own design specifies. Do not decline the experiment outright (O3): the checker-fidelity pre-test is cheap (harness reused nearly verbatim) and mechanistically distinct from #41's rejected coaching config, so foreclosing it forgoes a cheap test, not a costly one. Do not defer with no urgency (O4) as the disposition to this /decide invocation: the issue's own timing note ("owner's call; no urgency") governs *scheduling* of the full grid, not whether the accept/decline call itself gets answered now — CLAUDE.md requires a judgment call trigger `/decide` immediately, and "never fix a judgment call directly before deciding it" cuts against leaving this un-decided. This ADR's authorization is the disposition; execution timing remains loose (below).

## Justification
Unanimous panel signal: O2 scored 5/5/5 in round 1 (no reversal needed), O1 and O3 both scored 2/2/2 in round 1 — the panel converged without needing the round-2 reveal that O4 required. All three advisors' O2 crux is the same fact: this is verbatim the issue's own staged design (ISSUE-109.md:13-14) and matches ADR 0024's earn-before-spend / measure-before-improve discipline. The reversal premise common to O1/O2/O3 — "can false-accept be measured cheaply without running the full grid?" — is already answered yes: #41 measured checker false-accept on final artifacts standalone (ADR 0035 Act, `benchmarks/2026-07-10-tiered-execution-fullgrid/`), so the gate is not disguised full-grid cost.

## Assumptions
- [checkable] `benchmarks/2026-07-10-tiered-execution-fullgrid/` exists and contains reusable haiku-solo/sonnet-solo cells — owner: gate; result: verified 2026-07-14 (`ls benchmarks/`; directory present — note ISSUE-109.md:10 names it `2026-07-10-tiered-execution`, the actual dir has a `-fullgrid` suffix; same artifact, issue text is imprecise).
- [checkable] checker false-accept rate can be measured standalone, without executing the full routing pipeline — owner: gate; result: verified 2026-07-14 (ADR 0035 Act: "validator false-accepted 6/24" was computed from `results.jsonl` on the existing fullgrid run, not a new routing-specific run).
- [unverifiable] no checker variant plausibly beats the Sonnet judge's 25% false-accept rate (e.g., checklist-scored deterministic checks have already failed elsewhere) — REOPEN-IF a checklist/deterministic checker variant is attempted and also lands ~25%+ false-accept, at which point O3 (decline) becomes the standing call rather than O2's gate.

## Rejected alternatives
- O1 (run full grid now) — 2/5 unanimous; spends on a grid the pre-registered 25% false-accept threat could render moot before the gate is even checked.
- O3 (decline outright) — 2/5 unanimous; forecloses a test the issue itself frames as cheap and mechanistically distinct from #41's already-rejected coaching config.
- O4 (defer with no urgency, as the answer to this /decide) — split 3/2/2 in round 1, resolved via reveal; reads as sidestepping the accept/decline question this /decide was invoked to answer, even though the underlying issue text supports loose scheduling of *execution* (folded into this ADR's Decision, not left as a separate non-answer).

## Revisit triggers
- The checker-fidelity gate runs and no variant clears ~10-15% false-accept -> record the null per ADR 0024 D4; do not run the full grid.
- The checker-fidelity gate runs and a variant clears the bar -> proceed to the full routing grid under the decision rule sketch in ISSUE-109.md:16 (non-inferior frac-met, margin -0.05, clustered CI; median cost materially below sonnet-solo).
